examples.title: Winner Tree Construction Algorithm
examples.description: Algorithm to construct a winner tree (min selection tree) from k sorted sequences by comparing elements pairwise from bottom up.
examples.explanation: The construction algorithm uses a complete binary tree representation where leaves are stored from index k onwards. Starting from the lowest internal node, we compare the two children and store the smaller (winner) at the parent. This bottom-up approach ensures O(k) construction time since each of the (k-1) internal nodes requires exactly one comparison.

examples.title: Winner Tree Update Operation
examples.description: Update procedure after removing the winner (minimum element) from the root and replacing it with the next element from the same sorted sequence.
examples.explanation: The update operation propagates the new element from the modified leaf up to the root. At each level, both children are compared to determine the new winner. Since the tree height is ⌈log₂k⌉, the update requires at most log₂k comparisons, making it O(log k).

examples.title: Loser Tree Update Operation
examples.description: More efficient update algorithm for loser trees where internal nodes store the loser, requiring only one comparison per level.
examples.explanation: The key advantage of loser trees is that each internal node already contains the loser from the previous comparison. When a new element arrives, we only need to compare it with this stored loser to determine the winner that propagates upward. This eliminates the need to compare the new element with its sibling at each level, effectively halving the number of comparisons during updates.
